column2: Item Setup Instructions
Please use the following guidelines to help you complete the item setup process.
Workbook Structure
Excel Workbook - Every Excel workbook is category specific.
Excel Sheets - Each tab at the bottom of the workbook connects to a sheet; each sheet within the Excel workbook represents a subcategory.

The items in your catalog may fall across multiple categories (i.e. multiple workbooks) as well as multiple subcategories (sheets). Please make sure you fill out all the relevant workbooks and sheets to represent your catalog fully.
Template Layout

Within each sheet, each column has an attribute name and instructions. Each row gives you space for you to fill out the details related to a single item. For each sheet you can set up 2,000 items at a time.
First Row - The first row of each sheet represents the attribute name. Attributes can either be required (denoted by an asterisk) or optional
Second and third Row - Please do not unhide or delete the 2nd and 3rd row. Enter item information starting from row 4.
First Column (Generic Attributes) - Please do not unhide or delete. Enter item information starting from column B
Attributes Types
There are different types of attributes that are required or optional based on the type of product you are trying to create.
Generic Attributes - Generic attributes are the attributes that you need to fill out for your items regardless of the category or subcategory.
Category Attributes - Category attributes are the attributes that you need to fill out for that specific category.
Sub-Category Attributes - Sub-category attributes are the attributes that you will need to fill out for that specific sub-category only.
Required vs Optional Attributes

Required attributes must be filled out for item setup to succeed. Optional attributes are up to you. Be careful with optional attributes, however. If you provide information on one optional attribute and there are other inter-dependent attributes, you will have to fill them out as well. For example, if you provide shipping over-rides (which are optional), you must provide all 4 pieces of information related to a shipping over-ride.
Required attributes must  be filled out for item setup to succeed. Optional attributes are not required, but including more optional attributes will increase the discoverability of the item.

Some optional attributes come in groups; if a single attribute within a group is filled in, all of the attributes with the group must be filled in. For example, there are two optional attributes called MSRP-Unit and MSRP-Measure. If you set the MSRP-Unit attribute to USD, you must also put a value in the MSRP-Measure attribute.
Required Attributes - Required attributes are denoted with an asterisk (*) and are required to successfully set up your item.
Optional Attributes - Optional attributes are denoted with with (optional) at the end of the attribute. Optional attributes are not required to set up an item.
Some Field-Specific Comments
Product Tax Code (Marketplace Only) - This field is required. Valid values of the tax codes can be retrieved from the document posted on the partner portal.

Main Image-Main Image Url - This field contains the URL to an image for the item. It is highly recommened that this URL be a static URL ending in .jpg, .png, etc. If the URL  it has a query separator (?) or fragment separator (#) you will need to open a support ticket to whitelist your IP address, which may be a lengthy process.
Product Identifer-Product ID - This field contains the identifier specified in the field Product Identifer-Product ID Type. It cannot be a SKU - it must be a UPC, GTIN,  ISBN, ISSN or EAN.

Shipping Override-Is Shipping Allowed (Marketplace Only) - This field is one of ten groups of 4 fields. These groups of fields only need to be filled out if a general account-wide shipping method is to be disallowed for an item, or a shipping method is to be allowed for that item only. 
• One example would be a jewelry store that mainly sells rings and necklaces, but also sells sculptures that weigh over 500lbs. It typically would offer next-day air delivery for its smaller items, but would probably want to disallow this for the massive sculptures. The jeweler would then typically offer next-day air at a account level, but disallow it for the very heavy items.
• Since there are 10 groups of 4 fields, up to 10 allowed or disallowed shipping methods may be specified per item.

Variant Group ID, Primary Variant, Variant Attribute Names - these optional fields are used to support base-variant. All items in a base-variant group have their own row but are linked by having the same Variant Group ID, with the Primary Variant indicating which one displays by default, and the Variant Attribute Names referring to the attribute on which the group varies.

WPID - this is a field that you will only have once an item has been ingested. It refers to the internal Walmart ID, and if included, accelerates the processing of a later item update. For intial ingestions, leave it blank.
Product Name, Short Description, Long Description and Shelf Description
General Comments

Product Name and Shelf Description are visible to a potential buyer when an item appears in a search result. The Short Description becomes visible if the item page is displayed, and the Long Description displays when the potential buyer clicks on "Read more" below the Short Description.
HTML Tags
When providing a Long description, Shelf description or Short description, you have the option of providing either html or plain text.

HTML Tags - You should use HTML to provide enriched content in the form of bullet points for your descriptions. If you want to simply provide a paragraph, you do not need to use HTML. Bulleted Lists Sample HTML This is your first bold heading
  • Bullet 1
  • Bullet 2

This is your second bold heading
  • Bullet 1
  • Bullet 2
What would this look like? This is your first bold heading • Bullet 1 • Bullet 2 This is your second bold heading • Bullet 1 • Bullet 2 Multiple Paragraphs Sample HTML:

The George - Girls' Short Sleeve Polo Shirt will make a great addition to your daughter's uniform wardrobe.

This short-sleeved girls' shirt has a flat knit collar and cuff for a comfortable fit.
Walmart Search Optimization

Items on the Walmart.com will sell better if a few guidelines are followed. Please refer to Definitions worksheet for more details, but the most critical guidelines are:
  • Provide a unique Product Name for your item and repeat it once or twice in the Product Description
  • Provide a unique Product Description with 150+ words of informative, relevant content.
  • Provide high-quality images and rich content labeled with the Product Name.
